titleZh: 机械除草市场遇冷：信心不足成主要障碍
summaryZh: 本文分析了近期农业机械行业的多项重大变动：约翰迪尔停止喷雾机制造商Mazzotti业务、德国喷雾机企业Dammann申请破产、雷肯关闭荷兰工厂并整合除草机械生产。核心观点是市场低迷并非技术问题，而是农民因缺乏信心而推迟投资。对智慧畜牧行业而言，虽非直接相关，但反映了农业自动化设备推广面临的普遍市场信心挑战。
featuredReason: 虽然机械除草并非智慧畜牧核心领域，但该新闻揭示了农业自动化设备推广中普遍存在的市场信心问题，对智慧畜牧领域的技术落地和投资决策具有参考价值。
contentFull: Mechanical weeding loses momentum 12-06 | Weed/Pest control | Expert opinionLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint Bas Van Hattum Editor-in-chief TREKKERGerman machinery manufacturer Lemken recently announced that it will concentrate the production of hoeing machines and precision tine weeders at its site in Alpen, Germany. Photo: Koos GroenewoldManufacturers of sprayers, hoes and tine weeders are feeling the impact of farmers postponing investments. It is not the technology holding the market back, but a lack of confidence.In recent weeks, the mechanisation sector has seen a succession of striking announcements. John Deere has pulled the plug on sprayer manufacturer Mazzotti. German sprayer manufacturer Dammann has filed for insolvency, with the prospect of a restart. Lemken is closing its factory in Dinteloord, the Netherlands, whereLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintMore aboutcrop protectionhoeinvestmentmarket trendsmechanical weederadLEARN MORERelated articles25-06 | NewsFieldwork Robotics secures new funding to accelerate raspberry harvesting robotPremium24-06 | ArticleAustralian vegetable farm puts 2 weeding technologies to the test04-06 | NewsMazzotti era comes to an end within John DeerePremium26-05 | ArticleNo chemicals, no workers: The future of weed control is coming to Australia Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
喷雾机、锄草机和弹齿除草机制造商正感受到农民推迟投资带来的影响。阻碍市场的并非技术问题，而是缺乏信心。

近几周，机械化领域接连出现引人注目的公告。约翰迪尔已终止喷雾机制造商Mazzotti的业务。德国喷雾机制造商Dammann已申请破产，并有望重启。雷肯正在关闭其位于荷兰丁特尔奥德的工厂，该工厂此前生产锄草机和精密弹齿除草机。德国机械制造商雷肯最近宣布，将把锄草机和精密弹齿除草机的生产集中到其位于德国阿尔彭的工厂。
author: Bas Van Hattum
contentHtml: Manufacturers of sprayers, hoes and tine weeders are feeling the impact of farmers postponing investments. It is not the technology holding the market back, but a lack of confidence.
